title: Optimization:
content:
Optimization:
• Use incremental builds to speed up CI/CD.
• Profile performance on low-end devices (e.g., iPhone 8, budget Androids).
• Conduct accessibility audits early using axe and VoiceOver/TalkBack.
Challenges to Mitigate:
• Prioritize core gameplay (3D board, basic rules) to avoid scope creep.
• Schedule regular playtests to refine UX and performance.
Key Considerations for Mobile
1 Performance: Optimize for mid-tier devices (e.g., 2GB RAM, quad-core CPU). Use LOD, texture compression, and lightweight shaders. Test on devices like iPhone SE (2020) and Samsung Galaxy A51.
2 Storage: Keep initial app size under 200 MB, with eras downloaded on-demand via Addressables. Offer an “Offline Mode” with one era pre-installed.
3 Battery: Minimize background processes (e.g., disable AI inference when idle). Provide a low-power mode reducing effects and frame rate.
4 Retention: Hook players with quick onboarding (e.g., 1-minute Roman Villa tutorial). Use push notifications for multiplayer moves and daily challenges.
5 Monetization: Consider a freemium model with free access to one era and in-app purchases for additional eras or cosmetics. Avoid pay-to-win mechanics to maintain fairness.
